prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `e9bb6954`.
codex_response:
Implemented `e9bb6954` in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_e9bb6954/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_e9bb6954/verifier.py#L4), [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_e9bb6954/generator.py#L43), and [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_e9bb6954/notes.md#L1). The rule is: each isolated solid `3x3` square projects its color across the full grid on its center row and center column, and any crossing between different-colored projections is cleared back to `0`.

I used the Opus hint and discarded the Sonnet hint; the mismatch is documented in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_e9bb6954/notes.md#L1). The generator stays close to the official family by placing 1-3 isolated `3x3` seeds with distinct center rows/columns on medium rectangular grids, then adding only sparse singleton distractors while preventing same-color mergers into larger noise components. No new shared primitive was needed.

Validation passed end-to-end:
- `conda run --no-capture-output -n arc python -m arc2.shared.verify --task e9bb6954` -> `5/5` official examples passed.
- `conda run --no-capture-output -n arc python -m arc2.shared.build --task e9bb6954 --n-examples 1000` produced [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/e9bb6954/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/e9bb6954/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/e9bb6954/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/e9bb6954/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/e9bb6954/previews/preview_03.png).
- `./arc2_verify_generated_all.sh --task e9bb6954` -> `1000/1000` generated examples passed.
